records.ety: [L., 3d pers. sing. pres. of exire to go out. See Exeunt, Issue.]

defs: He (or she ) goes out, or retires from view; as, exit Macbeth.
notes: ☞ The Latin words exit (he or she goes out), and exeunt ( they go out), are used in dramatic writings to indicate the time of withdrawal from the stage of one or more of the actors.

defs: The departure of a player from the stage, when he has performed his part.
quotes: They have their exits and their entrances.

defs: Any departure; the act of quitting the stage of action or of life; death; as, to make one's exit.
quotes: Sighs for his exit, vulgarly called death.

defs: A way of departure; passage out of a place; egress; way out.
quotes: Forcing the water forth through its ordinary exits.
